text: The surgeon utilizes a combination of conventional, laparoscopic, and robotic ports, with robotic ports labeled R1 to R3. Dissection begins in a medial to lateral plane anterior to Todd's fascia, preserving the gonadal vessels and ureter. The inferior mesenteric artery and vein pedicle are sealed and divided. The dissection continues to the lateral limits, where adhesions with the colon are divided using a ligature. The ureter is identified again, and the colon is divided using a linear endogia stapler.

Robotic ports are placed under direct vision, and dissection is commenced in the total mesorectal excision (TME) plane using robotic instruments. The surgeon utilizes the robot's enhanced dexterity and three-dimensional magnified view to articulate instruments with precision. Dissection continues laterally down to the pelvic floor, with suction used to remove the smoke plume, further developing the lateral plane.

The surgeon identifies and divides the round ligament of the uterus on the left side, medializing the ureter. The dissection continues to isolate the left uterine artery, which is then diathermied and divided. The same procedure is performed on the right side, isolating and dividing the round ligament, medializing the ureter, and sealing and dividing the uterine artery.

The surgeon carefully develops the dissection plane between the bladder and uterus, proceeding down to the vault of the vagina. The anterior and lateral walls of the vagina are dissected, leaving the posterior wall intact as part of the en bloc specimen. A view from below is shown before gluteal flap reconstruction.
